AC: 15
STR: +20 HP: +50

Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 55

Sorry but yes the zone perma changes
QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2002 at 1:40 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248328364
45 posts
Sorry but once The Sleeper is released, he leaves the Tomb, rampages in Skyshrine then poofs forever. The 4 Warders also poof forever. The Tomb now only spawns golems and gargoyles that drop occassional Primal and Priceless weapons.

Yes, Verant would and DID make a zone perma change forever after the fisrt guild on your server releases The Sleeper, Kerafrym.
▲ Page top
RE: Sorry but yes the zone perma changes

# Jun 27 2002 at 4:46 AM Rating: Default

229 posts
Actually there is a quest involving turning in the ST key that seems to hint that the sleeper might be brought back.

I am not saying it is that way, but it could be.

QuoteReply
# Feb 06 2002 at 6:05 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593965854709
133 posts
After the Sleeper is woke on your server...no one will get one. This will not drop after that...so get it while you can if the sleeper is still sleaping on your server.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 10 2002 at 4:47 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) True, until the next time the server/zone goes down, like the next patch. Then you can go back in the zone like it had never been touched.
RE: One Problem...
QuoteReply
# Mar 01 2002 at 11:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766951827
4 posts
Once the Sleeper is up, the Warders' no longer spawn - which I would guess is what drops this. You are right, primal weapons will still come into the world, but at a very reduced rate. On the bright side, at least the mobs don't see invis after the Sleeper is up =)
▲ Page top
RE: One Problem...
QuoteReply
# Jun 11 2002 at 5:36 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766419186
88 posts
yes the ancient sentries do see invis even with the sleeper gone, they are keeping the prison ready it says when you enter the tomb now. the very last sentance is "for where wurmkind prevailed once, it shall prevail again" meaning there is a chance to get the sleeper back, rumor had it there was to be a quest out of vex thal, but since the mobs in vex thal are kos to everyone then it prolly doest exist in there, maybe PoP expansion will take the quest live, i dunno im just surmising here.
▲ Page top
let's make love
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2001 at 3:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768285314
4 posts
Arguments on who's better or who should drop dead is just absurd, we all got into this game without knowing which is the most "kick ***". We follow our style to pick a class, so there's just no reason to attack other classes. Let's not forget if one class doesn't perform well in the end game it's Verant that didn't balance the classes well. Let's stop flaming on each other's abilities nor declaring you own all other classes.
Monks in my opinion is great. Although I am a paladin am gifted w/ little down time and better tanking ability, but I admire the FD ability and recognize such ability is equally as useful as warrior's slash and hack. I respect all classes coz a Norrath filled w/ only warrior/enchanter/clerics would be just boring.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Oct 06 2001 at 11:29 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) guess what? all i have to say is this is a good item for who? ANY MELEE CLASS! just look at it stop whining about who is better and get on with gaming
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 12 2001 at 10:33 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) yes, monks do need ac, but they typically don't get it from items that give high ac themselves. this is why armor along the lines of Wu's fighting armor exists and is monk only, monks tend to get a large amount of their ac from agility, rather than ac from the item itself. so just stop screaming about how every item that weighs under 1 pound is "made for monks" i'm tired of hearing it.
#REDACTED, Posted: Mar 02 2002 at 10:19 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Edited, Sat Mar 2 22:21:48 2002
RE: monks can go screw
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2001 at 7:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593965965331
52 posts
Well, I don't disagree with the heading, monks need good loving jsut as much as the next class...

However, sir, you know jack all about the game. Show me one monk above level... um 15, wearing a serpentine bracer, and I'll say, oh yeah, AGI, DUDE, WOW! I think not... the AGI required to make up a single AC runs in the range of two to three... to make up this bracer, I'd need nearly 50 AGI... not that I, a monk, need resists or nothin.

Oh, by the way, how many monks do you know that raid Sleepers in Wu's Fighting? I'd just as soon pull up some lacy panties for AC and set to work.

Madalene Maindefer
51 Quellite of Bristlebane
▲ Page top
#Sigoniax, Posted: Nov 02 2001 at 10:24 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hahaha...panties, eh?...heheh
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 26 2001 at 9:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) i agree every decent item i've seen for rogues says this is perfect for monks. it is lighter then 1 pound, and BTW there isn't a better one for rogues according to this site.
RE: monks can go screw
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2001 at 8:13 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592757762465
4 posts
Uhmm that's pretty true, except for the fact that AC on items and bonuses from agi stack. And MOST of any monks AC does come from the AC on gear not agility (even with 200 or higher agi.) It's true that this is a good warrior item, but all the tanks i know in the lvl range of aquiring this item (and i am from Tallon so no UBER tanks here) already have 200 or higher str when in almost ANY type of group. The only reason i can see a warrior high enough to get this item wanting it is that they have pathetically low resists, or are one of the races that goes for high dex/proc'n weapons as king and needs a boost to their str.

Have you ever even seen the Wu's stats...they are great for pre-thrity-five or so (now that all the expansion gear is out), but saying that the fact that Wu's is monk only and all the pieces have a little agi on em and ***** for AC means that all monks should go for agi way before they go for AC and HP is completely moronic.

Next time you want a monk to even think about using his/her tstaff/fighter's staff/CF's think about the fact that most of the time we do enough dmg to take agro for a fair amount of time and 1 failed FD will result in death... Sure, we could not do dmg, but if you want an effective group with a monk in it the monk had better have good AC/hp.

And yes, this item IS made for monks. If it want't no drop i would say that it wasn't, but any warrior that is with people who can get this item could take the same people and get and item that is WAY better for em with the same people. Why take loot that some poor monk would use forever probly, when you can get something better in a matter of hours and will just end up destroying the item eventually anyway?
▲ Page top
RE: monks can go screw
QuoteReply
# Oct 01 2001 at 4:47 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965977775
44 posts
More monk items plz :)
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jul 06 2001 at 12:52 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Monks can go take a flying leap -- those stats scream warrior.
#Anonymous, Posted: May 15 2001 at 3:34 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) ok next time you complain and say "monks should have low ac" dont ask us to pull =)
#REDACTED, Posted: May 08 2001 at 10:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) you think someone is actually gonna take a monk with them to Sleeper's Tomb?

#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 22 2001 at 1:59 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) SKs rock as pick up group tanks. No other class can taunt better (maybe rangers, but nobody's ever seen a ranger tank for more than 15 seconds) :)
#REDACTED, Posted: Apr 12 2001 at 8:32 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This place in ment for monks so you all know
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 11 2001 at 10:03 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) u know i would like to say thanks to thoes that would step aside and not roll to give us monks a better shot at it.. even if it is 1 or 2 ppl that do out of 60 ppl that still makes the odds 1 in 58 instead of 60...Wt is important at high lvs so is str.. monks are now starting with vellos startting to have som decent top end armour.. kunark did the weapons and vellos id doing the armour=)
my opinion
QuoteReply
# Apr 03 2001 at 6:35 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I'll keep my warlord bracer and let my monk friends roll on this.. Why? well all the classes listed have atleast almost the same stat'd bracers except for monk. Plus if you haven't noticed monks have a very limited assortment of equipment to choose from, and from what I've noticed the "top of the line" monk equipment is all/all (warrior, rogue, ranger, paladin, sk all have "top of the line" equipment that is class specific).

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 16 2001 at 12:53 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Ok. I play a monk. Mind you my monk is lvl 8 which is no where near getting this bracer. I play a monk because I didn't want to play a druid because lets face it, almost everyone is a druid. I didn't want to play a necro cause it's not my style of play. Mind you I have nothing against druids or necros, they are honorable classes to persue. Their just not my gaming style. I need to be where the action is. I want to feel the splatter of blood on my opponent when i hit him with my fist, staff, or greatstaff.
Rogues

ok this is probly the best melee class wrist in the game by a long way this should have no real specified only for monk or whatever there is nothing better for any of the classes

#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 13 2001 at 8:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Hey guess what *************** is a warrior item! We can top all you loserz anytime. Ha monks, hybrdis, and rogues! Oh my! Im really scared. Gimme a break. I dont see why VERANT made room for gay *** classes like yours. Warriors 4 life!
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 15 2001 at 10:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) SUCK IT!!!
#Anonymous, Posted: Feb 14 2001 at 6:46 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) LOL What an ***. Heres a day in the life of a warrior:
RE: F A G S ! ! ! !
QuoteReply
# Feb 14 2001 at 8:03 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This moron is not a good representation of the warrior class, or of a human being for that matter... However, I do take exemption to your post. After playing EQ for over a year and a half, the only character I've taken above 20th lvl is a warrior. Not because it requires no thought but because I enjoy being in the thick of it. Being a *good* warrior does take a lot of concentration and awareness. When you drop a 600 pt nuke on a MOB, I need to be ready to whack and taunt him off of you. I have to watch every caster in the group to make sure there isn't a baddie using them for a punching bag. Casters are just as dependant on tanks (not just warriors, but all tanks) to keep them alive as we are dependent on you casters to keep us healed and the crowd control and the like.

For the record:
a) I do not beg for SoW.
b) I do not beg for Ports.
c) I do not beg for Invis.
d) I do not beg for Rezs.
e) And I only attack a blue if I'm near a zone ;P
If some one offers any of the above to me, I will not turn them down and always (at least try to) donate.

My 2cp.

Griznik Skullstrike
48th Circle Warrior
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Aug 04 2002 at 9:35 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) shut up dOOd
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 16 2001 at 10:29 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) it took you over a year and a half to get just one (1) character over lvl 20, sheesh, just must not know how to play, or have 7 lvl 19 chars. . . . .
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 03 2001 at 7:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I agree with Griznik, it's not fair to base your opinion of all warriors on this guy. I am not an idiot (at least I don't think I am) but I too choose to be a warrior because I enjoy being in thick of it all, and I love duel weilding. So please, just because of one moron, don't think us all that stupid :P
